Class Robot

java.lang.Object
Robot
public class Robot extends Object
Represents the robot's state: where it is, which way it faces, and how many moves it has made.

The robot is a domain object that knows only about itself. It does not know about the grid, rocks, keyboard input, or drawing. That separation keeps the code easier to test and reason about: the RobotController checks the grid and then tells the robot to move or turn; the robot just updates its own position and direction.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Robot(String name, Position startPosition, Direction startDirection)
    Creates a robot with the given name, starting position, and initial facing direction.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Direction
    getDirection()
    Returns the direction the robot is currently facing.
    int
    getMovesTaken()
    Returns how many successful forward moves the robot has made (turns are not counted).
    String
    getName()
    Returns the robot's display name.
    Position
    getPosition()
    Returns the robot's current cell on the grid.
    String
    getStatusLine()
    Builds a one-line status string for debugging or plain-console output (e.g.
    void
    moveForward()
    Moves the robot one step forward in its current direction and increments the move counter.
    Position
    peekForwardPosition()
    Computes the position one step ahead in the current facing direction, without moving.
    void
    turnLeft()
    Turns the robot left (counter-clockwise) without changing position.
    void
    turnRight()
    Turns the robot right (clockwise) without changing position.

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Robot

      public Robot(String name, Position startPosition, Direction startDirection)
      Creates a robot with the given name, starting position, and initial facing direction.
      Parameters:
      name - display name (e.g. "Robo")
      startPosition - cell where the robot begins
      startDirection - direction the robot faces at the start

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the robot's display name.
      Returns:
      the name (e.g. "Robo")

    • getPosition

      public Position getPosition()
      Returns the robot's current cell on the grid.
      Returns:
      current position (row, column)

    • getDirection

      public Direction getDirection()
      Returns the direction the robot is currently facing.
      Returns:
      NORTH, EAST, SOUTH, or WEST

    • getMovesTaken

      public int getMovesTaken()
      Returns how many successful forward moves the robot has made (turns are not counted).
      Returns:
      the number of forward moves

    • peekForwardPosition

      public Position peekForwardPosition()
      Computes the position one step ahead in the current facing direction, without moving. The controller uses this to check whether that cell is inside the grid and not a rock before calling moveForward().
      Returns:
      the position that would be reached by moving forward one step

    • moveForward

      public void moveForward()
      Moves the robot one step forward in its current direction and increments the move counter. Should only be called after the controller has verified the move is legal (inside grid, no rock).

    • turnLeft

      public void turnLeft()
      Turns the robot left (counter-clockwise) without changing position.

    • turnRight

      public void turnRight()
      Turns the robot right (clockwise) without changing position.

    • getStatusLine

      public String getStatusLine()
      Builds a one-line status string for debugging or plain-console output (e.g. "Robo is at (2, 3), facing North, successful moves: 5").
      Returns:
      a human-readable status line
